segments.text: India versus Pakistan. Space and missile race. Who wins?
Space missions are the biggest gap. India has launched well over one hundred missions. Pakistan has launched only a few satellites.
Launch capability is another huge difference. India can launch from its own pads. Pakistan cannot match that.
Satellite count and domestic space infrastructure also strongly favor India.
Missile range is where the rivalry becomes more balanced. Both sides field nuclear-capable systems.
But India has broader depth across short, medium, and longer-range missile programs.
Pakistan still maintains a credible deterrent with rapid-response systems aimed at the regional balance.
So in space technology and missile depth together, India has the wider capability base.
Pakistan remains strategically relevant. But by the numbers, India wins this race.
